articles.author: Enrico de Lazaro
articles.title: Scientists Reconstruct One of Oldest Known Neanderthal Communities - Sci.News
articles.description: Mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) from eight fossils found in Stajnia Cave in Poland reveals a tight-knit group of Neanderthals who lived about 100,000 years ago, offering one of the clearest genetic snapshots yet of a single community in prehistoric Europe.
